af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Multi-channel e-commerce order management and shipping platform. HTTP Basic Auth with API key as username and API secret as password — unusual but documented. 40 req/min rate limit is tight for high-volume order processing agents. Order import from stores is async — freshly synced orders not immediately queryable. Carrier accounts must be connected via ShipStation dashboard before API can use them. Label creation follows a multi-step workflow. Tags and custom fields help filter orders but require advance configuration. Webhooks for order updates available.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. HTTP Basic Auth with API key+secret — credentials base64-encoded per HTTP spec. SOC2 and PCI-DSS certified (shipping integrations handle payment-adjacent data). No fine-grained per-resource scoping — key has full account access. Credentials via environment variables (avoid hard-coding). PCI-DSS compliance relevant for order data handling. No RBAC beyond account-level access.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2/PCI-DSS. Stable v2 API. Carrier availability varies — some carriers may be unavailable during peak periods. Async order import means agents need polling logic for freshly synced stores. Retry guidance not formally documented. 40 req/min requires agent throttling. Offset-based pagination. Generally reliable as core fulfillment infrastructure.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Search engine for internet-connected devices — ports, services, CVEs, banners, geolocation. Single API key passed as query parameter or via SDK. IMPORTANT: Free tier returns maximum 1 result per query — nearly unusable for agent workflows, paid tier required. Query credits consumed even on empty results — agents can burn credits on bad queries. Data is not real-time (crawl-based, days to weeks old). Facet queries and filter syntax non-obvious for agents. Scan API requires separate on-demand credits. SDKs in Python, Ruby, Java, Go, JavaScript.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key — no scope granularity (all endpoints accessible per account tier). Security-focused company but ironically limited API key controls. No sensitive personal data handled — internet infrastructure metadata. API key via environment variables recommended (query parameter exposure in logs is a concern). No formal compliance certs documented — primarily a research/security tool.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Shodan status page. Stable REST API with backward compatibility. Data freshness varies — crawl timestamps available per result. Credit consumption on failures creates retry cost risk. Retry guidance not documented. Free tier extreme limitations mean agents need to test with paid account. Offset-based pagination. Generally reliable for security research.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Shopify Admin API (REST + GraphQL) — industry-leading e-commerce platform. Custom app access tokens (simple for agents) vs public app OAuth 2.0. IMPORTANT: REST API being deprecated in favor of GraphQL — new functionality only in GraphQL. GraphQL rate limits use cost points, not req/sec — agents must budget cost carefully. Rate limits are per-app-per-store — multi-store agents share limits. Webhooks require HMAC-SHA256 verification. Cursor-based pagination. Excellent documentation and error messages. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory for all API access. Access tokens for custom apps, OAuth 2.0 with fine-grained scopes for public apps (read_products, write_orders, read_customers, etc.). SOC2 Type II, PCI-DSS Level 1, GDPR, CCPA compliant. Excellent scope granularity via OAuth — least-privilege access achievable. Webhook signatures for integration security. Secrets via environment variables. Industry-leading e-commerce security posture with regular third-party audits.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Shopify status page and enterprise SLA. Stable API with versioned REST API and evolving GraphQL. REST-to-GraphQL migration is well-documented. Retry guidance documented. GraphQL cost calculation documented. Cursor-based pagination on GraphQL. Webhook retry logic documented. REST deprecation timeline requires planning. Very reliable as core commerce infrastructure for millions of merchants.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Shortcut (formerly Clubhouse) project management REST API. Simple API token per workspace member in Shortcut-Token header — no scopes, full workspace access. Workflow state IDs are workspace-specific — must query states before transitioning stories. Stories, epics, milestones are different entity types with separate endpoints. Search API has limited field coverage — some complex queries require listing and client-side filtering. Webhooks can be large payloads. Archive vs delete are distinct operations. Cursor-based pagination. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API token with full workspace access — no resource-level scoping. Token compromise exposes all workspace data. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. No per-operation permission control. Token via environment variables. Webhook delivery verification available. Simple security model appropriate for internal team tooling but lacks enterprise RBAC.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2. Stable v3 REST API with OpenAPI spec. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. Workflow state IDs are stable within a workspace but require initial query. Webhook payloads have size variability. Generally reliable project management platform with consistent API behavior.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. SignNow e-signature platform API. auth_complexity_score=72 — OAuth2 bearer tokens that expire in 24 hours require token refresh for long-running agents; refresh tokens available. Document field coordinates specified in pixel positions — precision required. Template creation from document is multi-step. Webhook configuration requires specifying exact event types. HIPAA compliance requires explicit Business Agreement and specific plan tier. Documentation less comprehensive than DocuSign or PandaDoc. Rate limits not documented. SDKs in PHP, Python, Java, Node, .NET.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. OAuth2 bearer tokens with 24-hour expiry — secure token rotation. SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA, ESIGN, UETA compliant. No fine-grained scope control on API keys. Business Agreement required for HIPAA compliance. Token storage must be secure given 24-hour expiry and sensitive document content. Client credentials via environment variables.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. Stable REST API. Token refresh required for agents running longer than 24 hours. Document signing is async — webhook or polling needed for completion. Retry guidance not documented. Rate limits undocumented — unpredictable throttling risk. Offset-based pagination. Less battle-tested at enterprise scale than DocuSign. Generally reliable for SMB e-signature workflows.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Sinch multi-channel communications platform (owns Mailgun, Mailjet) covering SMS, voice, WhatsApp, email. auth_complexity_score=68 — service-specific API keys don't work across products; SMS key doesn't work for voice; agents need separate credentials per service. Sinch has acquired many companies (Mailgun, Mailjet, MessageBird) — products have different API styles and auth mechanisms. Phone number provisioning separate from messaging. Country-specific carrier regulations affect deliverability. Webhook URLs are per-product. SDKs in Python, Node, Java, .NET, PHP.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory across all services. Service-scoped application key+secret pairs provide some isolation between SMS, voice, and messaging services. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. Acquired products (Mailgun, Mailjet) have separate security postures. Credentials per service via environment variables. No fine-grained per-operation scoping within services.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Sinch status page, SOC2. Core SMS/voice stable. IMPORTANT: Post-acquisition integration of Mailgun, Mailjet, and MessageBird means different products have varying stability. Cursor-based pagination on some services. Retry guidance not documented. Country-specific routing can cause delivery failures. Number provisioning is a dependency step. Products acquired from other companies may have legacy reliability characteristics.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. SingleStore (formerly MemSQL) HTAP cloud database management API. auth_complexity_score=65 — dual auth model: Management API uses JWT tokens (short expiry requiring refresh) + database connections use MySQL username/password. Separate credentials for management vs data access. MySQL compatibility is high but not complete — some features behave differently. Columnstore vs rowstore table type selection critical for performance. Shared tier has significant compute limitations. Rate limits not documented. No SDK — use raw HTTP or generate from OpenAPI spec.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. JWT tokens for Management API + MySQL standard auth for data access. SOC2 Type II, ISO27001, HIPAA, GDPR compliant — strong compliance for enterprise data workloads. JWT token short expiry requires agent token refresh logic. MySQL credentials via environment variables. Org-level scoping on management operations. Enterprise compliance appropriate for sensitive analytics workloads.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2/ISO27001. Stable MySQL-compatible data layer. JWT token expiry adds retry complexity for management operations. HTAP architecture is complex — columnstore/rowstore behavior must be understood for reliable performance. Shared tier limitations affect reliability testing. No retry guidance documented. Offset-based pagination. Generally reliable for enterprises with proper configuration.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 55). Universal documentation-to-AI-knowledge converter — processes docs sites, GitHub repos, PDFs into Claude skills, RAG formats, and IDE context files. Core scraping works without API keys; LLM enhancement requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, or OPENAI_API_KEY. auth_complexity_score=82 (keys needed only for optional AI features). Version 3.1.0-dev indicates active development with potential breaking changes. OCR for scanned PDFs requires additional system dependencies. GitHub API rate limits apply during repo analysis. Conflict detection between docs and code is novel but accuracy unverified. Rate limits not documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for LLM API calls (when AI features enabled). LLM API keys needed only for optional AI enhancement features. No auth on MCP server itself. Community project — dependency audit required before production use. LLM API keys via environment variables. Content scraped from external sites is sent to LLM APIs if enhancement is enabled — data privacy consideration for proprietary documentation. No formal security audit.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — no SLA. 3.1.0-dev version indicates pre-stable — breaking changes expected. OCR requires additional system dependencies not bundled. GitHub API rate limits can interrupt large repo analysis. Processing is batch-based, not real-time. Conflict detection accuracy unverified. Limited production track record given dev status. Best for experimental/exploratory knowledge building rather than production pipelines.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 65). Lightweight MCP server that loads Claude-style SKILL.md files and exposes them as tools to non-Claude MCP clients (Codex, Copilot, Cursor). auth_complexity_score=100 (no auth needed — local tool). IMPORTANT: Explicitly described as 'experimental proof-of-concept' — not production-ready. Skills can execute arbitrary scripts — no sandboxing without Docker. SKILL.md format is Claude-specific convention, not a universal standard. Stdio, HTTP, and SSE transports supported. Docker provides the only isolation mechanism. 369 stars indicates early adoption.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local MCP server — no external network calls for core operation. CRITICAL SECURITY CONCERN: No auth, no sandboxing, skills can execute arbitrary scripts. Developer documentation advises treating skill code as untrusted. Docker volume mounts are the only security boundary. Community experimental project — verify all dependencies. No credential handling needed but arbitrary code execution risk is significant. Not suitable for untrusted skill sources or shared environments.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — no SLA. Explicitly labeled experimental proof-of-concept. 369 stars and early-stage adoption. SKILL.md format tied to Claude conventions — may change. Stdio, HTTP, SSE transport options provide flexibility. Error handling is basic. Docker isolation adds operational complexity. Appropriate for experimentation and cross-platform skill sharing in dev environments, not production.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 65). Hybrid SAST + dead code detection for Python, TypeScript, and Go. Core static analysis requires no credentials; AI remediation requires LLM API key (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Ollama). auth_complexity_score=88 (LLM key optional). Go analysis requires separate standalone binary beyond pip install. AI auto-PR creation requires GitHub token with write access. Framework-aware false-positive reduction (Flask, FastAPI, pytest fixtures). CI/CD integration via GitHub Actions. Performance benchmarks are self-reported. No rate limits (local tool).

security_score_components.security_notes: Local analysis tool — no external network for core static analysis. LLM API keys (optional) for AI remediation via environment variables. GitHub token (optional) for auto-PR features. Open source Python — audit dependencies before use. Code being analyzed stays local unless AI remediation sends snippets to LLM API. No formal security audit. Taint analysis for security scanning is a sensitive capability — verify false negative/positive rates.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — no SLA. Early project with growing adoption. Go analysis binary is a separate dependency. Framework-aware exclusions require correct project structure detection — misconfigured projects may produce incorrect results. LibCST-based safe removal can fail on unusual Python syntax. AI remediation depends on external LLM API availability. Performance benchmarks against Vulture only, not independently verified. idempotent analysis — same code produces same results.
